i18n_common_patterns.overview: Common code patterns and examples for working with i18n translation files. These patterns demonstrate proper structure and techniques for various translation scenarios.

structure.description: TypeScript file with default export (most common in React projects)
structure.usage: Used in React/TypeScript projects with i18next or similar
Used in Node.js or older JavaScript projects
Simple projects or static translation files

example.note: Note the space before the exclamation mark in French

rules.rule: Variable names must remain unchanged across all languages
Maintain spacing around variables for readability
Variable position can change to match target language grammar

pattern.description: Multiple variables in a single string

rules.rule: All variables must be present in translation
Order can change to fit target language grammar
Do not translate formatting type names

syntax.separator: Use pipe (|) to separate singular and plural forms

example.note: Russian has four forms based on count ending

rules.rule: Translate only text content, not tag names or attributes

example.code: termsText: "By signing up, you agree to our Terms of Service"
termsText: "Al registrarte, aceptas nuestros Términos de Servicio"

rules.rule: Keep href attributes unchanged unless they point to localized pages

example.code: richContent: "For help, visit our <a href='/help'>Help Center</a> or <strong>contact support</strong>"
richContent: "Para ayuda, visita nuestro <a href='/help'>Centro de Ayuda</a> o <strong>contacta soporte</strong>"

translations.translation: time: { now: "ahora mismo", minutesAgo: "hace {{count}} minuto" | "hace {{count}} minutos", hoursAgo: "hace {{count}} hora" | "hace {{count}} horas", daysAgo: "hace {{count}} día" | "hace {{count}} días", weeksAgo: "hace {{count}} semana" | "hace {{count}} semanas" }

example.code: dateHelp: "Ingrese la fecha en formato DD/MM/YYYY"

pattern.note: Adapt date format references to match target locale conventions

pattern.example: export default { actions: { save: "Save", cancel: "Cancel", delete: "Delete", edit: "Edit", create: "Create", update: "Update", submit: "Submit", close: "Close", confirm: "Confirm", viewDetails: "View Details", download: "Download", upload: "Upload", share: "Share", copy: "Copy", move: "Move" } };

usage_tips.tip: Always preserve the exact structure when translating
Use search_files to find similar patterns in existing files
Maintain consistent terminology across related keys
Test interpolation by imagining actual values in place of variables
Consider text length - some languages require more space
Keep button labels concise - they need to fit in UI
